page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ebmg7a.64xlarge is a high-capacity instance from the ecs.ebmg7a family, built on the x86_64 CPU architecture. It features 256 dedicated physical cores and 256 vCPUs, operating with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration that ensures no hyperthreading overhead. The instance is equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for data persistence. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 64 Gbps, facilitating rapid data transfer across cloud resources. Operating under the KVM hypervisor, the dedicated CPU allocation guarantees that compute resources are fully isolated and consistently available without noisy-neighbor interference.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.ebmg7a.64xlarge offers high resource density for massive enterprise workloads, consolidating multiple smaller workloads or running large-scale parallel applications on a single host. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs focuses the hardware profile entirely on raw, multi-core CPU processing and memory capacity. This makes the instance highly suitable for large-scale general-purpose applications, high-concurrency web serving, enterprise databases, and distributed compute tasks that require high network throughput and dedicated CPU execution.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ebmg7a.64xlarge is a massive, dedicated instance from the ecs.ebmg7a family, featuring 256 physical cores and 256 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, it eliminates hyperthreading overhead to deliver consistent, isolated compute performance. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, maintaining a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 64 Gbps. It contains no local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. This hardware profile is optimized for large-scale enterprise applications, high-concurrency web serving, and distributed compute workloads requiring high network throughput and dedicated CPU execution.
